title: BG16RFPR001-1.010 New Models in Production by SMEs
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: Procedure budget EUR 96.3M; company aid depends on enterprise category and aid regime
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises introducing new or improved production, process, goods, or service models.
description: A Bulgarian grant procedure for SMEs implementing new models in production or business processes. It can fit tech startups with existing revenue and a concrete investment project for software, equipment, patents, designs, or licenses.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises
Companies registered by the procedure's required reference date
Applicants with sufficient recent revenue and a project implemented in Bulgaria
whoCannotApply: Idea-stage founders without a registered SME
Applicants without the required sales history
Projects that do not introduce a new model at company level
eligibleCosts: Patents, utility models, industrial designs, and licenses
Selected employment costs where allowed by the procedure
evaluationCriteria: Fit with Innovation Strategy for Smart Specialisation areas
Quality of the new product, service, or process model
sourceNotes: MIG updated the procedure after ISUN interruptions; the current official deadline was 5 June 2026 at 16:30.

title: BG16RFPR001-1.011 Introduction of Innovations by SMEs in Local Initiative Group Territories
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
geography: Bulgaria - approved Local Initiative Group territories
amount: Procedure budget EUR 19.98M; individual grants EUR 15,000 to EUR 102,500, up to 75% intensity
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian SMEs located on Local Initiative Group territories implementing innovations aligned with the Smart Specialisation Strategy.
description: A local Bulgarian grant route that complements community-led local development strategies. It supports SMEs on approved LIG territories that introduce new or improved products, processes, or innovation services.
whoCanApply: Existing SMEs registered as traders or cooperatives
Applicants operating on an eligible Local Initiative Group territory
Projects introducing innovation in Smart Specialisation priority areas
whoCannotApply: Companies outside eligible LIG territories
Applicants without SME status
Projects without a clear innovation component
eligibleCosts: Implementation costs allowed by the specific LIG strategy
evaluationCriteria: Location and eligibility under the local strategy
sourceNotes: MIG public-consultation page lists EUR 19,976,187.59 budget and EUR 15,000 to EUR 102,500 individual grant range; the 2026 PCIE work programme indicates multiple deadlines through October 2026.

title: BG16RFPR001-1.012 Digitalization of Enterprises
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: BGN 5,000 to BGN 50,000 grant aid, up to 70 percent intensity
eligibilitySummary: Closed Bulgarian procedure for micro and small enterprises investing in ICT, digital marketing, ERP, CRM, and cybersecurity solutions.
description: A closed but useful reference procedure for Bulgarian digitalization grants. Similar future calls are likely to require clear SME eligibility, specific ICT purchases, and simple cost justification.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian micro and small enterprises meeting the procedure rules
Companies purchasing eligible ICT and digitalization solutions
Applicants able to apply through ISUN before the deadline
whoCannotApply: Companies outside Bulgaria
Medium or large enterprises if excluded by the procedure
Projects without eligible ICT or cybersecurity/digitalization activity
eligibleCosts: Production, logistics, and management process optimization tools
evaluationCriteria: Fit of ICT solution to business needs
sourceNotes: MIG page tracks contracts and documents; public summaries list the 26 September 2025 deadline.

title: BG16RFPR001-2.003 Green Technologies in SMEs
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: Procedure budget BGN 162.3M; grants BGN 150,000 to BGN 800,000, up to 70% intensity
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian SMEs introducing green technologies and circular-economy models that reduce waste, resource use, or environmental impact.
description: A Bulgarian SME grant for circular-economy investments such as waste reduction, recycled or bio-based inputs, reduced packaging, longer product life, and sustainable products or services.
whoCanApply: Existing Bulgarian SMEs registered as traders or cooperatives
Companies with sufficient recent sales history under the call rules
Applicants introducing green technologies in eligible economic activities
whoCannotApply: Idea-stage teams without an operating SME
Projects without measurable green or circular-economy outcomes
Applicants outside eligible sectors or revenue thresholds
eligibleCosts: Machinery and equipment for green technologies
Intangible assets including software, patents, designs, and licenses
Services linked to the green technology implementation
evaluationCriteria: Regional aid and de minimis compliance
sourceNotes: MIG announced the draft procedure for public consultation; the 2026 PCIE work programme lists May-August 2026 timing and BGN 150,000 to BGN 800,000 project grant range.

title: BG16RFPR002-1.005 Small Innovative Grants for SMEs
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / RIDST 2021-2027
amount: Fixed grant BGN 14,710 for one service or BGN 29,420 for two services
eligibilitySummary: Closed Bulgarian fixed-grant route for SMEs buying science-intensive innovation services from eligible knowledge providers.
description: A reference procedure for Bulgarian SMEs needing focused help from universities, research organizations, competence centers, Sofia Tech Park R&D association, or scientific infrastructure providers.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises
Companies needing innovation services from eligible knowledge providers
Applicants with a defined technological or business-model problem
whoCannotApply: Companies without SME status
Applicants seeking general consulting unrelated to innovation
Projects without an eligible knowledge provider
eligibleCosts: New product, process, service, or business-model support
sourceNotes: MIG programme page lists BG16RFPR002-1.005 under R&I and Digitalisation for Smart Transformation.

title: BG16RFPR002-1.011 Participation of Bulgarian Organisations in Institutionalised European Partnerships
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / RIDST 2021-2027
geography: Bulgaria with Horizon Europe partnership projects
amount: Procedure budget EUR 27.79M; maximum grant EUR 5.11M per concrete beneficiary
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian organisations already approved in Horizon Europe institutionalised partnerships may receive complementary national grant support.
description: A direct-grant route for Bulgarian beneficiaries participating as coordinator or partner in EU institutionalised partnerships under Horizon Europe, including chips, clean hydrogen, rail, health, aviation, and smart networks partnerships.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian legal entities, public or private
Organisations approved for funding by the European Commission in eligible institutionalised partnerships
Bulgarian coordinators or designated Bulgarian partners with a signed EC agreement
whoCannotApply: Applicants without an approved Horizon Europe partnership project
Projects outside the listed institutionalised partnerships
Activities beyond the eligible R&D or feasibility scope
eligibleCosts: Activities eligible under the relevant Horizon Europe partnership and RIDST rules
requiredDocuments: ISUN application with qualified electronic signature
Evidence of Horizon Europe approval and contract
evaluationCriteria: Fit with Horizon Europe partnership rules
sourceNotes: MIG lists three submission windows ending 15 June 2026, 16 November 2026, and 31 May 2027; this record tracks the first active window.

title: BG16RFPR002-1.018 Support for Bulgarian Innovative Enterprises with Seal of Excellence Projects
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / RIDST 2021-2027
amount: National grant support for Bulgarian companies with qualifying Seal of Excellence projects
eligibilitySummary: Bulgarian innovative enterprises with projects that received an EIC Seal of Excellence quality label.
description: A Bulgarian national support route for companies whose strong EU innovation proposals received a Seal of Excellence but need national funding to execute the project.
whoCanApply: Bulgarian innovative enterprises
Applicants holding a relevant Seal of Excellence
Companies able to implement the project in line with national procedure rules
whoCannotApply: Applicants without a qualifying Seal of Excellence
Projects that materially differ from the awarded proposal
Companies that fail national eligibility checks
eligibleCosts: Project activities consistent with the Seal of Excellence proposal
Eligible national budget categories defined in ISUN documents
sourceNotes: EUMIS page lists the procedure and 22 June 2026 deadline.

title: Burgas Municipality Support for Startup Entrepreneurs and Young Innovators
provider: Burgas Municipality / Business Incubator Burgas
amount: Up to EUR 2,556.46 (BGN 5,000); up to 50% project support, or 100% for student teams
eligibilitySummary: Local Burgas startup and youth innovation support for newly created firms, teams, and student entrepreneurs implementing first-time local ideas.
description: A municipal grant and mentoring route that helps early founders and young innovators in Burgas validate and launch practical solutions for local economic, social, or environmental challenges.
whoCanApply: Individuals and legal entities, including micro and small enterprises registered up to three years before application
Teams of at least two people with a shared business idea
Burgas students over 16 years old
whoCannotApply: Projects not implemented for the first time in Burgas
Applicants outside the municipal programme scope
Projects without practical local benefit
eligibleCosts: Mentoring, training, office, technical, and logistics support
requiredDocuments: Business idea and local impact statement
sourceNotes: Burgas Municipality announced the 2026 session with a 13 April 2026 deadline; keep as a watch-list item for future municipal intakes.

title: EIC Accelerator 2026
provider: European Innovation Council
geography: EU and Horizon Europe associated countries
amount: Grant up to EUR 2.5M plus equity investment for scale-up financing
eligibilitySummary: Single startups and SMEs with high-risk, high-impact innovations that can scale in Europe and globally.
description: A highly competitive EU instrument for breakthrough innovations that need grant support, investment, or blended finance to move from validated technology toward market scale.
whoCanApply: Single startups or SMEs established in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe associated country
Applicants with a high-risk innovation and credible route to international scale
Teams able to explain technology, market, IP, and commercialization strategy
whoCannotApply: Projects with routine product development and limited innovation risk
Large companies applying alone
Companies that cannot show credible ownership or access to the core technology
eligibleCosts: Prototype validation, pilots, testing, certification, and scale-up preparation
Commercialization and investment-readiness work where aligned with the call
sourceNotes: EIC funding page lists Accelerator 2026 as open with 2026 cut-off dates.

title: EIC STEP Scale Up
provider: European Innovation Council
geography: EU and Horizon Europe associated countries
amount: Equity investments around EUR 10M to EUR 30M to catalyze large funding rounds
eligibilitySummary: Scaleups with strategic deep-tech innovations and investor pre-commitments for large funding rounds.
description: A scale-up investment route for companies that can become global leaders in strategic technologies and need major EU-backed equity support.
whoCanApply: High-growth SMEs, startups, small mid-caps, or eligible scaleups
Companies with strategic technologies and credible investor interest
Teams preparing large investment rounds
whoCannotApply: Idea-stage teams
Companies without scale-up traction or serious investor engagement
Projects that are not tied to strategic technology sectors
evaluationCriteria: Ability to catalyze a larger investment round
sourceNotes: EIC funding page lists STEP Scale Up as open with 2026 cut-off dates.

title: EIT Urban Mobility Financial Support To Startups Open Call 2026
provider: EIT Urban Mobility
geography: EU and Horizon Europe associated countries
amount: Startup capital and support package for selected urban mobility startups
eligibilitySummary: European mobility startups with scalable solutions and evidence of pilots, MVP, or market traction.
description: A sector-focused EIT route for startups improving urban mobility, logistics, electrification, shared transport, mobility data, and related climate or digital infrastructure.
whoCanApply: Startups incorporated in eligible European countries
Companies building urban mobility solutions
Teams with an MVP, pilot, or early traction
whoCannotApply: Companies outside the mobility scope
Pure consulting or non-scalable service projects
Teams without a product or pilot path
eligibleCosts: Pilots with cities or mobility partners
sourceNotes: EIT opportunity listing shows Financial Support To Startups Open Call 2026 with a 16 November 2026 deadline.

title: EIT Venture Incubation Program
provider: European Institute of Innovation and Technology
geography: EU and eligible European innovation ecosystems
amount: Mentoring, venture-building support, ecosystem access, and possible follow-on opportunities
eligibilitySummary: Early teams and startups developing digital or innovation-led ventures that need incubation and market validation.
description: An EIT opportunity suited to early founders who need structured incubation, mentor access, and validation before pursuing larger grants or investment.
whoCanApply: Early-stage startup teams
Founders with a technology-enabled venture idea
Teams able to commit to the incubation timeline
whoCannotApply: Mature scaleups looking only for large grant funding
Projects unrelated to innovation or digital business creation
Teams without a clear problem, customer, or proposed solution
sourceNotes: EIT opportunity listing shows Venture Incubation Program with a 9 June 2026 deadline.

title: Eurostars September 2026 Call
provider: Eureka Network / Eurostars
geography: Participating Eurostars countries, including Bulgaria through national funding bodies
amount: National funding rates apply; Bulgarian SMEs may receive public support for eligible R&D costs when national budget is available
eligibilitySummary: Innovative SMEs leading international collaborative R&D projects with partners from participating countries.
description: Eurostars funds market-oriented international R&D projects led by innovative SMEs. It is strongest for startups that need cross-border technical collaboration and can define a product, process, or service with commercial potential.
whoCanApply: Innovative SMEs leading an international consortium
Consortia with independent partners from at least two participating countries
Projects with civilian, market-oriented R&D and clear commercialization potential
whoCannotApply: Applicants without an international partner
Projects without meaningful R&D work
Pure sales, distribution, or routine implementation projects
eligibleCosts: Materials and subcontracting where allowed nationally
sourceNotes: Eureka publishes Eurostars calls and national funding information by country.

title: Hydrogen Value Chain Capacity
provider: Programme Regional Development 2021-2027 / Just Transition territories
geography: Stara Zagora and linked transition municipalities in Bulgaria
amount: Listed budget BGN 291.8M; project grant limits to be clarified in call documents
eligibilitySummary: Regional hydrogen value-chain support for SMEs, public bodies, municipalities, universities, and research organisations in Bulgarian just-transition territories.
description: A large regional-transition route for green hydrogen production, storage, distribution, use, and digital management assets in Stara Zagora and linked municipalities.
whoCanApply: SMEs in eligible transition territories
Ministry of Energy or its enterprises
Eligible municipalities
Universities and research organisations including BAS
whoCannotApply: Applicants outside the eligible territorial scope
Projects unrelated to hydrogen value-chain capacity
Applicants without technical and implementation capacity
eligibleCosts: Electrolysers, renewable energy, water treatment, transformers, and inverters
Buffer tanks, compression or liquefaction systems, safety and monitoring systems
Hydrogen charging stations, logistics platforms, and tracking systems
Fuel cells, drive systems, and demonstration vehicles
SCADA, ERP, HMI, IoT, AI, sensors, cloud infrastructure, and labs
requiredDocuments: Consortium or public-body documents where relevant
sourceNotes: Euro-Programs and the PRR indicative work programme list the hydrogen value-chain route and eligible transition territories; verify official opening before marking open.

title: Ideas Powered for Business SME Fund 2026
provider: EUIPO / European Commission, with Bulgarian Patent Office information
amount: IP reimbursement vouchers; as of June 2026 patent and plant-variety vouchers remain available
eligibilitySummary: EU-based SMEs, including Bulgarian SMEs, can apply for IP reimbursement vouchers before filing eligible IP actions.
description: A light EU grant scheme helping SMEs protect intellectual property. It is useful for startups preparing patents, trade marks, designs, or plant-variety protection, subject to voucher availability.
whoCanApply: SMEs established in the EU or Ukraine
Business owners, employees, or authorised representatives applying on behalf of the SME
Applicants who wait for grant decision before claiming reimbursement
whoCannotApply: Non-SME applicants
Applicants seeking reimbursement before receiving the voucher decision
Requests for voucher categories whose funds are exhausted
eligibleCosts: Community plant variety fees under Voucher 4
Other IP services when the relevant voucher is available
requiredDocuments: VAT certificate or company registration evidence
Proof of payment for reimbursement claim
sourceNotes: EUIPO states the 2026 SME Fund runs from 2 February to 4 December 2026; Bulgarian Patent Office republishes the opportunity for Bulgarian SMEs.

title: Intervention II.G.3 Rural Non-Agricultural Investments - Crafts
provider: State Fund Agriculture / Strategic Plan for Agriculture and Rural Areas 2023-2027
amount: Budget BGN 5.87M; eligible project costs BGN 29,337 to BGN 782,320, up to 50% aid
eligibilitySummary: Self-employed craftspeople and micro-enterprises in Bulgarian rural areas investing in craft activities.
description: A small rural grant route for craft businesses and micro-enterprises, relevant for makers, artisan manufacturing, and rural creative production with equipment or digitalization needs.
whoCanApply: Individuals registered under the Crafts Act as self-employed craftspeople
Micro-enterprises
Applicants seated in eligible rural municipalities
whoCannotApply: Applicants outside rural territories
Projects outside eligible craft activities
Mobile renewable-energy installations and other excluded costs
eligibleCosts: Client websites, office equipment, software, and licenses
sourceNotes: Euro-Programs catalogue lists the 2025 crafts intake; use as a discovery record and verify future intakes against DFZ/SEU.

title: Intervention II.G.3 Rural Non-Agricultural Investments - Production
provider: State Fund Agriculture / Strategic Plan for Agriculture and Rural Areas 2023-2027
amount: Budget BGN 91.45M; eligible project costs BGN 29,337 to BGN 782,320, up to 50% aid
eligibilitySummary: Micro-enterprises and eligible farmers in Bulgarian rural areas investing in non-agricultural production activities.
description: A rural development grant for local production businesses outside agricultural Annex I products, including equipment, construction, renewable energy, software, websites, and mobile applications.
whoCanApply: Farmers registered for at least 24 months with required economic size
Micro-enterprises and sole traders
Applicants seated in eligible rural municipalities
whoCannotApply: Applicants outside rural territories
Companies above micro-enterprise size where excluded
Tourism accommodation projects and other excluded activities
eligibleCosts: Client websites, mobile applications, software, and licenses
requiredDocuments: Investment project where construction is included
sourceNotes: DFZ official intake page and Euro-Programs catalogue list the 2025 production intake; keep as a rural local-grant reference and monitor for future calls.

title: Intervention II.G.3 Rural Non-Agricultural Investments - Services
provider: State Fund Agriculture / Strategic Plan for Agriculture and Rural Areas 2023-2027
amount: Budget BGN 95.83M; eligible project costs BGN 29,337 to BGN 782,320, up to 50% aid
eligibilitySummary: Micro-enterprises and eligible farmers in Bulgarian rural areas diversifying into non-agricultural services and goods.
description: A rural development grant for local service businesses that complement or diversify the rural economy, including equipment, construction, renewable energy, software, websites, and mobile applications.
whoCanApply: Farmers registered for at least 24 months with required economic size
Micro-enterprises
Applicants seated in eligible rural municipalities
whoCannotApply: Applicants outside rural territories
Projects outside eligible service or goods activities
Tourism accommodation projects and other excluded activities
eligibleCosts: Client websites, mobile applications, software, and licenses
requiredDocuments: Company and farmer registration evidence where applicable
sourceNotes: DFZ official intake page and Euro-Programs catalogue list the 2025 services intake; keep as a rural local-grant reference and monitor for future calls.

title: Introduction of Industry 4.0 Technologies in Enterprises
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: Budget BGN 105.98M; grants BGN 150,000 to BGN 850,000
eligibilitySummary: Small and medium Bulgarian enterprises investing in Industry 4.0 software, equipment, cybersecurity, and standards.
description: A reference PCIE digitalization grant for higher-level Industry 4.0 investments, including data analytics, cloud or edge, IIoT, AI/ML, additive manufacturing, simulation, AR/VR, cybersecurity, and standards.
whoCanApply: Small and medium enterprises registered by the call reference date
Companies meeting 2024 sales thresholds
Applicants investing in eligible Industry 4.0 technologies
whoCannotApply: Micro-enterprises where excluded
Applicants below revenue thresholds
Projects that do not introduce at least one eligible Industry 4.0 technology
eligibleCosts: SaaS licenses during eligible implementation period
Production and technological equipment for Industry 4.0
sourceNotes: Euro-Programs catalogue lists the call parameters; EUMIS evaluation criteria confirm grant thresholds and Industry 4.0 technology scoring.

title: Sofia Municipal Guarantee Fund - FISP for Innovative and Startup Enterprises
provider: Sofia Municipal Guarantee Fund for SMEs
amount: Municipal guarantee scheme supporting bank loans for innovative and startup SMEs in Sofia
eligibilitySummary: Sofia-based innovative or startup SMEs that need a municipal guarantee to access bank financing for an economically viable project.
description: A financial-instrument route rather than a grant. It is useful for startups and SMEs in Sofia that have a viable project but insufficient collateral for a bank loan.
whoCanApply: Innovative or startup SMEs in Sofia Municipality
Companies with an economically viable project
Applicants seeking bank financing with insufficient collateral
whoCannotApply: Projects without bankability or repayment capacity
Applicants outside the municipal guarantee scheme rules
Teams looking only for non-repayable grants
eligibleCosts: Project costs financed by partner-bank loans
Investment and growth expenses accepted by the guarantee scheme
Working-capital or investment uses allowed by the partner bank
sourceNotes: OGF Sofia lists FISP as a programme for financing innovative and startup enterprises in Sofia and reports aggregate guaranteed loans.

title: Sofia Municipality Europe Programme 2026
provider: Sofia Municipal Council / Sofia Municipality
amount: Municipal grant programme; funding parameters are set in the Sofia Municipal Council 2026 programme documents
eligibilitySummary: Sofia civic-sector projects for sustainable development, green urban environment, participation, tourism, prevention, and links with Bulgarian communities abroad.
description: A Sofia municipal grant programme for civic and community projects. It is not a pure startup programme, but it is relevant for social-impact and local civic-tech initiatives that work with municipal priorities.
whoCanApply: Eligible civic-sector organisations under the programme rules
Applicants working with Sofia municipal priorities
Partnerships with municipal structures where required by a priority area
whoCannotApply: Pure commercial startup projects
Projects outside Sofia Municipality's priority areas
Applicants unable to execute and report by the programme timetable
eligibleCosts: Green urban environment and healthy lifestyle initiatives
Cultural, tourism, community, prevention, and inclusion activities
evaluationCriteria: Fit with one of the 2026 priority areas
sourceNotes: Sofia Municipal Council approved the 2026 priority areas and timetable; application was planned for February-March 2026.

title: Sofia Municipality Social Innovations Programme 2026
provider: Sofia Municipality
amount: Up to EUR 12,782 per project; 2026 programme budget up to EUR 209,629
eligibilitySummary: Sofia-based non-profits and community centres implementing short social innovation projects for vulnerable groups; commercial activity is not supported.
description: A municipal social-innovation grant for short projects introducing new ideas, processes, or models in Sofia's social policy practice, especially for vulnerable groups.
whoCanApply: Non-profit legal entities based in Sofia Municipality
Narodni chitalishta based in Sofia Municipality
Applicants proposing a new-for-Sofia social innovation
whoCannotApply: Commercial companies and cooperatives
Political parties, trade unions, and religious organisations
Projects supporting economic activity rather than social innovation
eligibleCosts: Project delivery and reporting costs allowed by the procedure
requiredDocuments: Submission by paper/electronic carrier or signed email
evaluationCriteria: New social innovation for Sofia Municipality
sourceNotes: Sofia Municipality lists the 2026 application period, EUR 12,782 project cap, and EUR 209,629 programme budget.

title: Support for Clean and Resource-Efficient Technologies of Strategic Importance (STEP)
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / PCIE 2021-2027
amount: Planned budget BGN 58.7M; grants BGN 150,000 to BGN 7,000,000, up to 70% intensity
eligibilitySummary: Existing SMEs and larger enterprises investing in clean and resource-efficient technologies within the EU STEP strategic technology scope.
description: A planned Bulgarian STEP-aligned route for strategic clean and resource-efficient technologies and related value chains, aimed at reducing EU strategic dependencies.
whoCanApply: Existing SMEs and large enterprises registered as traders or cooperatives
Applicants investing in STEP-scope clean or resource-efficient technologies
Companies with a credible industrial or value-chain strengthening project
whoCannotApply: Projects outside STEP clean/resource-efficient technology scope
Applicants without implementation and financing capacity
Routine efficiency upgrades without strategic technology relevance
eligibleCosts: Construction and installation works where eligible
Personnel costs allowed by the call
sourceNotes: Included in the 2026 PCIE indicative work programme with September-December 2026 timing and BGN 150,000 to BGN 7,000,000 grant range.

title: VISA-1.001 Startup Visa Certificate for High-Tech or Innovative Project
provider: Bulgarian Ministry of Innovation and Growth / ISUN
amount: Certificate route supporting long-term residence permit applications; no direct grant funding
eligibilitySummary: Non-EU founders seeking a Bulgarian certificate for a high-tech or innovative project as part of a residence pathway.
description: A Bulgarian administrative support procedure that certifies high-tech or innovative projects for non-EU founders. It does not provide funding, but it can help foreign founders establish and operate an innovative business in Bulgaria.
whoCanApply: Third-country founders outside the EU
Founders proposing a high-tech or innovative project in Bulgaria
Applicants able to submit the required documents through ISUN
whoCannotApply: Applicants seeking direct grant funding
Projects without a credible innovation or high-tech basis
EU citizens who do not need this residence route
eligibleCosts: Administrative preparation and company setup are the founder's responsibility
requiredDocuments: Evidence of high-tech or innovative nature
sourceNotes: EUMIS page lists application period from 2 January 2026 to 31 December 2029.
